## Pick #1 — Score: 8/10

**SpaceX Eyes $1.8 Trillion IPO Valuation**
*Google News - Technology + 2 more*
[Read the full article →](https://news.google.?oc=5)
**Live Pick:** true

**Why it matters:**
A SpaceX IPO at $1.8 trillion would make it one of the most valuable public offerings in history, cementing Elon Musk's grip on both the AI-satellite economy and the broader narrative of private space commercialization. Musk's signal that the AI satellite infrastructure will lean on existing tech isn't just a cost story — it's a power move that tells Wall Street the margins are already there. This is the moment private space stops being a moonshot bet and starts trading like a utility.

**Hook:**
SpaceX is reportedly eyeing an IPO at a $1.8 trillion valuation — which would make it more valuable than most countries' GDPs / Musk just told investors the AI satellite stack runs on tech they've already built, meaning the money printer is closer than anyone thought / This isn't a startup going public — it's a space empire demanding to be priced like one
